figures.caption: Figure 4. (a,c) Surface charge ratio of the CoFeB/NiFe bilayer bottom surface and (b,d) its frequency shift as a function of the applied magnetic field. (a) and (b) corresponds to a NiFe sublayer thickness dNiFe = 25 nm, and (c) and (d) for dNiFe = 25 nm. A wave vector magnitude |kx| = 40 rads/µm was taken for calculations.

Figure 5. (a-c) Frequency shift extreme values ∆f ext at (b-d) the corresponding wave vector kext x of a CoFeB/NiFe bilayer, as a function of the applied magnetic field. The CoFeB sublayer thickness is set to dCoFeB = 25 nm, whereas three different thicknesses of the NiFe sublayer were considered.

Figure 2. (a) Coercive field as function of Permalloy thickness dNiFe. Helical equilibrium states of the bilayer CoFeB/NiFe, with CoFeB (NiFe) thickness dCoFeB = 25 nm (dNiFe = 25 nm), and KCoFeB = 500 kJ/m3. The equilibrium helical states are shown in (b) for dNiFe = 25 nm and (c) for dNiFe = 47 nm.

Figure 3. Dispersion relation of an insulated and homogeneously saturated NiFe layer at different propagation directions ϕr and zero applied magnetic field µ0H = 0 mT of thickness (a) dNiFe = 25 nm and (b) dNiFe = 47 nm, respectively. (c) Damon-Eschbach dispersion relation of an insulated and homogeneously saturated CoFeB layer of thickness dCoFeB = 25 nm and in-plane uniaxial anisotropy KCoFeB = 500 kJ/m3, at different magnetic fields applied in −ẑ direction. (d) Dispersion relation and (e) frequency shift ∆f = (f [kx] − f [−kx]) of CoFeB/NiFe system with dNiFe = 25 nm. (f) Dispersion relation and (g) frequency shift ∆f = (f [kx]−f [−kx]) of CoFeB/NiFe system with dNiFe = 47 nm. (h-i) and (j-k) Mode profiles at |kx| = 40 rad/µm of and a set of applied field µ0H ∈ {0, 90, 111, 150} mT for dNiFe = 25nm and dNiFe = 47 nm, respectively, where top (bottom) line of modes are for kx = 40 rads/µm (kx = −40 rads/µm).
